source: Low Hct is associated with anemia, a frequent finding in psoriasis.
Anemia (low Hgb) is common in psoriasis due to chronic inflammation and potential iron deficiency.
Low MCH (mean corpuscular hemoglobin) is associated with microcytic anemia, a potential finding in psoriasis.
Low MCHc (mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration) is associated with microcytic anemia, a potential finding in psoriasis.
Low MCV (microcytosis) can be seen in psoriasis due to iron deficiency anemia.
Elevated platelet count (thrombocytosis) can occur in psoriasis as a reactive response to inflammation.
Low RBC count (anemia) is common in psoriasis due to chronic inflammation and potential iron deficiency.
Elevated RDW can indicate increased red blood cell production variability, which may be seen in psoriasis due to inflammation.
Elevated WBC count (leukocytosis) can be seen in psoriasis due to inflammation and potential infection.
